introParagraphs: How honest are your dating experiences, really?

Dating doesn't have to be a game, it can be as simple as getting to know another human being. Discovering compatibility with another person is only possible when we show up as our true selves, but we have to be willing to let our true selves be seen.
This dating expansion pack can be added to the original WNRS card game. Each player alternates between asking and answering each question. How do you win? Stay honest.
perception: How many relationships do you think I've been in? How many times do you think I've been in love? Explain.
Just by looking at me, what would you think I do for a living?
What was the first thing you noticed about me when we met in person?
What do you think my type is? Describe in detail.
What do you think I was like in high school? Explain.
What do you think my drink of choice is?
Do you think I'm still on good terms with my exes? Why or why not?
connection: When's the last time you ghosted someone and why?
What are you feeling a lot of lately?
What about me intrigued you besides my physical appearance?
Do you have any unconventional opinions when it comes to romantic relationships?
What do your friends know about me, if anything?
I know I really like someone when ________.
What part of your job energizes you the most? Leaves you the most drained?
Has sex ever been a deal breaker in your past relationships? Explain.
How has your view on love evolved over time, if at all?
How do you define "dating"?
Describe your worst heartbreak. What did it teach you?
Have you ever considered an open relationship? Why or why not?
Who in your life do you feel most yourself around? Why?
Which one of your parents' personality traits do you want to keep? Let go of?
What is something new you've learned about yourself recently?
What's the hardest part about dating you?
What are you overthinking right now?
What have you tolerated from others in the past that you no longer have space for?
How emotionally available do you currently feel? Explain.
What has being single taught you about yourself?
What was the worst date you've ever been on and why?
What about you has felt repeatedly misunderstood by others, if anything?
What's a non-negotiable in your life?
What did the people who raised you teach you about love? How has that shaped your views on love today?
reflection: What song should I listen to on my way home?
Based on what you've learned about me, what do you think I'm looking for romantically?
On a scale of 1–10 how "emotionally available" do I seem? Explain.
What mannerisms of mine stood out the most to you, if any?
How would you describe our date in one word?
If you were to write my dating profile bio, what would it say?
What part of my personality isn't showcased online?
What do you find most attractive about me that isn't physical?
wildcards: Write down your 3 favorite physical features of your partner and read them out loud. Both players.
Write down your top 2 relationship deal breakers. Both players. Compare.
Write down your favorite song lyric you can think of off the top of your head. Compare and explain.
Share something you typically wouldn't on a first date. Both players.
Give each other tattoos with the nearest pen or marker. 30 seconds.
Write down the top 3 most important qualities in a romantic partner. Both players. Compare.
Think of the most underrated show streaming currently. On the count of 3, say your answers out loud. Both players.
Look through each other's social media. Pick your favorite photo. Compare and explain.
Write your partner a text outside of your comfort zone at this end of this date. Both players.
Ask literally anything. Permission to go there.
digDeeper: Say more—what makes you feel that way?
When did you first notice that?
How does that show up in your day to day?
What would change if the opposite were true?
finalPrompts: Before you go: what's one thing you're grateful you shared tonight?
What's one word for how you're leaving this conversation?
What's something you want to say that we haven't covered yet?
What do you want to remember about each other from tonight?
Write a private handwritten note to each other. Fold them, exchange, and read later on your own—like the Final Card in the box.
